commonIssues:
The MCD online portal is difficult to navigate, with problems such as being unable to set up accounts, forms getting rejected despite correct data entry, or duplicate slip errors.

How to fix:

  • Use the MCD Online Portal on Chrome browser in incognito mode to avoid caching issues.
  • For duplicate slip errors, visit the crematorium office to get a new unique slip number.
  • Ensure you apply in the correct MCD zone (North/South/East/Shahadra) where the death actually occurred, not where the family resides.
  • Helpline: 1800-11-0093 (MCD Toll-Free)

Families get stuck when the hospital refuses to apply (stating it was a "brought dead" case) and the MCD rejects the application directing them back to the hospital.

How to fix:

  • Request the hospital's Medical Record Officer (MRO) to issue a "Brought Dead" certificate officially confirming the hospital's position.
  • Take this certificate to the MCD Zone office directly, as the MCD can process such cases on receiving the Brought Dead certificate.
  • If both refuse, file a complaint at the Delhi District Collector's office or approach the Delhi State Legal Services Authority (DSLSA) for free legal aid.
  • DSLSA helpline: 011-23385816

Difficulties arise when trying to provide documentation for deaths from the distant past, especially searching for manual records before 2007.

How to fix:

  • Visit the MCD Records Room of the relevant zone office and request a manual records search.
  • Obtain a Non-Availability Certificate (NAC) from the MCD if the record is not found.
  • With the NAC, approach the Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) to issue a court order for delayed registration.
  • MCD Records enquiry: MCD Online Portal

Deaths not reported within 21 days incur late fees and require complex procedures (affidavits/magistrate orders), often confusing residents.

How to fix:

  • 21 days to 30 days: Apply at the MCD office with a late fee of ₹25.
  • 30 days to 1 year: Apply with an affidavit and permission from the Chief Registrar (MCD Commissioner office).
  • After 1 year: Obtain an order from the SDM of the concerned district and then apply at the MCD zone office.
  • Apply online: MCD Online Portal | Helpline: 1800-11-0093

Correcting mistakes (name/date) in an issued certificate requires a lengthy process involving a judicial affidavit, and sometimes a correction letter from the Medical Record Officer.

How to fix:

  • Obtain a Judicial/Notarised Affidavit on stamp paper stating the error and the correct information.
  • Get a correction letter from the hospital's Medical Record Officer (MRO) if the error originates from medical records.
  • Submit both documents to the MCD Zone office where the certificate was issued.
  • Track correction at MCD Online Portal or contact 1800-11-0093.
